video_title: North of North | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
I echo the people saying it’s great to see a story that focuses on the Inuit people. They are a part of who we are here in North America. I’m sure there’s a great comedy that you could base off of them and their culture. Cool!

The trailer, however, makes the protagonist’s ethnicity look like the only original thing this show has to offer. I’m pretty sure I’ve seen most of the plot outline a million times.

Motherhood is terrible, especially if you become a mother when your body is most capable of motherhood—then it’s REALLY a bad idea.

Marriage is also terrible. It’s always the man’s fault that the woman is not happy. The only way for a woman to improve her life is to divorce her husband and engage in a full-time profession.

When she does, she will discover how awesome she is. She will get the appreciation she never got at home, and a dude who is much better than her husband will pursue her.

Oh yes, let’s not forget about white people. White people are stupid, ignorant and almost always bigoted. Every other race is cultured, tolerant and enlightened.

So, a show about the Inuit people? Cool! Sign me up.

A show that follows a predictable plotline we’ve seen over and over since the 1970s? No thanks. I’ll pass.

comment_text: I don't know who is reading these comments, but I would like to ask a question: was this filmed in or near Kotzebue, Alaska? It sure looks like it. I spent a summer there working in a medical clinic/small hospital operated by the Maniilaq Assocation. I visited the local cultural museum, spoke with the native leaders, tasted both whale blubber and seal meat, among other memories. I still have my Maniilaq Association T-shirt from 1993. Anyway, good luck with the series.

video_title: North of North | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: Watched season 1 already and would highly recommend! Completely different setting for someone that has watched way too many shitty American sitcoms in his life, a breath of fresh air! One special mention for the songs they choose for the episodes, you'd almost think the is a The CW show, that was pretty much the only thing they were good at. Huge bonus points also for having some Inuktitut songs & covers here and there as well!
